title: Concrete, light item
summary: A standard expansion plug is usually enough for a light item in concrete.
steps: Use a concrete drill bit and matching plug.
warnings: Check for hidden cables or pipes.

title: Concrete, light item — full guide
summary: Use the right hole size, remove dust and tighten carefully.
dontDo: do not drill without checking installations
do not use an oversized drill bit

title: Concrete, medium weight
summary: Use a stronger fixing with spare capacity.
warnings: Do not mount heavy items without certainty.

title: Concrete, medium weight — full guide
summary: Use stronger plugs and several mounting points.

title: Brick
summary: Choose a plug for solid or hollow brick.

title: Brick — full guide
summary: Check brick type before choosing the plug.
dontDo: do not drill near an edge

title: Drywall, light item
summary: Use a plug made for plasterboard.

title: Drywall — full guide
summary: Avoid overload and use the right drywall plug.
dontDo: do not hang heavy items on board only

title: Wood
summary: A proper wood screw is usually enough.
steps: Make a pilot hole if needed.

title: Wood — full guide
summary: Match screw length and diameter to the material.
dontDo: do not use a short screw
do not screw near an edge

title: Risky mounting
summary: For heavy items, do not guess. Check capacity or call a professional.
steps: Do not start without checking wall.

title: Risky mounting — checklist
summary: For heavy items, the app does not replace a professional.
steps: Do not mount above beds or walkways.

title: Fixing selection
summary: Start with wall type and object weight.
warnings: Do not mount heavy items without certainty.

title: Fixing selection — checklist
summary: Safe mounting starts with wall, weight and correct fixing.
